---
title: "Aim 1: Cross-sectional Analyses"
author: Michelle Li
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warnings = FALSE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here::here())
```

```{r setting}
library(broom)
library(car)
library(patchwork)

source('workflow/scripts/config.R')

lastvisit_dat <- readRDS(file.path(DATA_CLEANED_PATH, "PREVENTAD_lastvisit.rds"))
```

# Model fit

```{r regression}
models <- tribble(~model, ~predictors,
                  "model0", "age + sex + educ",
                  "model1", "age + sex + educ + GRS + apoe",
                  "model2", "age + sex + educ + GRS + apoe + family_history",
                  "model3", "age + sex + educ + apoe + family_history + score_libra2",
                  "model4", "GRS + apoe + family_history + score_cogd") 

all_preventad <- expand_grid(
  outcome = BIOMARKERS_TRANS,
  models
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    eq = glue("{outcome} ~ {predictors}"),
    res = map(eq, ~lm(as.formula(.), data = lastvisit_dat)),
    dataf = map(res, tidy),
    mod = map(res, glance)
  )
```

```{r regression-results}
res_all_preventad <- bind_rows(select(all_preventad, model, outcome, dataf)) %>%
  unnest(dataf) %>%
  filter(
    str_detect(term, '^GRS|^family_history|^score_libra2|^score_cogd|^age|^sex|^educ|^apoe')
  ) %>%
  arrange(outcome, term, estimate) %>%
  mutate(
    # Significance flag
    sig = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, TRUE, FALSE),
    
    # Confidence intervals
    conf.low = estimate - (std.error * 1.96),
    conf.high = estimate + (std.error * 1.96),
    
    # Exponentiated values for log-transformed outcomes 
    exp_est = case_when(
      str_detect(outcome, "_log") ~ exp(estimate),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    exp_lci = case_when(
      str_detect(outcome, "_log") ~ exp(conf.low),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    exp_uci = case_when(
      str_detect(outcome, "_log") ~ exp(conf.high),
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    pct_change = case_when(
      str_detect(outcome, "_log") ~ (exp_est - 1) * 100,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),
    
    # Result category
    res = case_when(
      sig == TRUE ~ "Significant",
      sig == FALSE ~ "Not Significant"
    ),
    stars = case_when(
      p.value > 0.05 ~ "", 
      between(p.value,  0.01, 0.05) ~ "*",
      between(p.value, 0.001, 0.01) ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***"
    ),
    
    # Categorize predictors
    category = case_when(
      term == "age" ~ "Demographics",
      term %in% c("sexMale", "sex") ~ "Demographics",
      term == "educ" ~ "Demographics",
      str_detect(term, "^apoe") ~ "Genetics",
      term == "GRS" ~ "Genetics",
      str_detect(term, "^family_history") ~ "Family History",
      term == "score_libra2" ~ "LIBRA2",
      term == "score_cogd" ~ "CogDrisk",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  ) %>%
  # Round numeric values
  mutate(across(c(estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value, 
                  conf.low, conf.high, exp_est, exp_lci, exp_uci, pct_change), 
                ~round(., digits = 3)))
```

## Results - Table
```{r results='asis'}
# Term label mapping for nicer display
term_labels <- c(
  "age" = "Age",
  "sexMale" = "Sex (Male)",
  "educ" = "Education",
  "GRS" = "GRS",
  "apoee4+" = "APOE (e4+)",
  "apoee2+" = "APOE (e2+)",
  "family_history1 FDR + ext" = "Family History (1 FDR + ext)",
  "family_history2+ FDR" = "Family History (2+ FDR)",
  "family_history2+ FDR + ext" = "Family History (2+ FDR + ext)",
  "score_libra2" = "LIBRA2 Score",
  "score_cogd" = "CogDrisk Score",
  "educ" = "Education"
)

# Row order: Demographics -> Genetics -> Family History -> CRS
term_order <- c(
  "Age", "Sex (Male)", "Education", "BMI",
  "GRS", "APOE (e4+)", "APOE (e2+)",
  "Family History (1 FDR + ext)", "Family History (2+ FDR)", "Family History (2+ FDR + ext)",
  "LIBRA2 Score", "CogDrisk Score"
)

for (i in models$model){
  model_title <- case_when(
    i == "model0" ~ "Model 0: Biomarker ~ Demographics",
    i == "model1" ~ "Model 1: Biomarker ~ Demographics + Genetics",
    i == "model2" ~ "Model 2: Biomarker ~ Demographics + Genetics + Family History",
    i == "model3" ~ "Model 3: Biomarker ~ Demographics + Genetics + Family History + LIBRA2",
    i == "model4" ~ "Model 4: Biomarker ~ Genetics + Family History + CogDrisk",
    TRUE ~ paste("Model:", i)
  )
  
  res_table <- res_all_preventad %>% 
    filter(model == i) %>%
    mutate(term = ifelse(term %in% names(term_labels), term_labels[term], term)) %>%
    mutate(term = factor(term, levels = term_order)) %>%
    arrange(term) %>%
    mutate(term = as.character(term)) %>%
    select(outcome, model, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value, stars) %>% 
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = outcome,
      names_sep = "_",
      values_from = c(estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value, stars)
    ) %>%
    select(-model) %>%
    gt() %>% 
    tab_spanner(
      label = md("**AB42/40**"),
      columns = ends_with("ab_ratio")
    ) %>%
    tab_spanner(
      label = md("**GFAP**"),
      columns = ends_with("gfap_log")
    ) %>%
    tab_spanner(
      label = md("**NfL**"),
      columns = ends_with("nfl_log")
    ) %>%
    tab_spanner(
      label = md("**ptau181**"),
      columns = ends_with("ptau181_log")
    ) %>%
    tab_spanner(
      label = md("**ptau217**"),
      columns = ends_with("ptau217_log")
    ) %>%
    tab_spanner(
      label = md("**ptau217/AB42**"),
      columns = ends_with("ab42_ratio_log")
    ) %>%
    cols_label(
      starts_with("estimate")~ md("*Beta*"),
      starts_with("std.error")~ md("*SE*"),
      starts_with("statistic")~md("*Statistic*"),
      starts_with("p.value")~ md("*p.value*"),
      starts_with("stars")~ ""
    ) %>%
    tab_header(title = md(paste0("**", model_title, "**"))) %>%
    opt_align_table_header(align = c("left"))
  
  print(res_table)
}
```


## Results - Plot

```{r regression-plot}
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 10
#| out-width: "100%"

for (i in models$model){
  model_title <- case_when(
    i == "model0" ~ "Model 0: Biomarker ~ Demographics",
    i == "model1" ~ "Model 1: Biomarker ~ Demographics + Genetics",
    i == "model2" ~ "Model 2: Biomarker ~ Demographics + Genetics + Family History",
    i == "model3" ~ "Biomarker ~ Demographics + Genetics + Family History + LIBRA2",
    i == "model4" ~ "Biomarker ~ Genetics + Family History + CogDrisk",
    TRUE ~ paste("Model:", i)
  )
  
  # Determine color variable for this model
  # use_term_colors <- i %in% c("model0", "model1")
  
  color_palette <- c(
    "Demographics" = "black",
    "Genetics" = "#0073C2FF",      # Blue (JAMA palette)
    "Family History" = "#EFC000FF", # Yellow (JAMA palette)
    "CogDrisk" = "#CD534CFF",      # Red (JAMA palette)
    "LIBRA2" = "#CD534CFF"         # Red (JAMA palette)
  )
  
  # Prepare data with conditional color variable
  plot_data <- res_all_preventad %>%
    filter(model == i) %>%  
    mutate(sig = factor(sig, levels = c(TRUE, FALSE))) %>%
    mutate(term = factor(term,
                         levels = c("score_cogd", "score_libra2",
                                   "family_history2+ FDR + ext", "family_history2+ FDR", "family_history1 FDR + ext",
                                   "GRS", "apoee4+", "apoee2+",
                                   "educ", "sexMale", "age"))) %>%
    mutate(term = fct_recode(term,
                             "Age" = "age",
                             "Education" = "educ",
                             "Sex (Male)" = "sexMale",
                             "APOE e4+" = "apoee4+",
                             "APOE e2+" = "apoee2+",
                             "1 FDR + ext" = "family_history1 FDR + ext",
                             "2+ FDR" = "family_history2+ FDR",
                             "2+ FDR + ext" = "family_history2+ FDR + ext",
                             "CogDrisk" = "score_cogd",
                             "LIBRA2" = "score_libra2")) 
  
  res_plot <- ggplot(plot_data,
    aes(y = term, x = estimate, group = term, xmin = conf.low, xmax = conf.high, 
        color = category, alpha = sig)  
  ) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +  
  geom_errorbarh(linewidth = 1, position = position_dodge(width = 0.5), height = 0.2) +  
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = 2) +
  scale_alpha_manual(
    values = c(`FALSE` = 0.3, `TRUE` = 1),  # Not sig = transparent, sig = opaque
    labels = c(`FALSE` = "Not Significant", `TRUE` = "Significant"),
    name = "Significance"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = color_palette,
    name = "Predictor Type"
  ) +  
  facet_wrap(~outcome, scales = "free_x", ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    x = "Beta (95% CI)",
    y = "Predictor",
    title = model_title
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, angle = 0, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9)
  )
  
  print(res_plot)
  # Save each plot
  # filename <- paste0("PREVENTAD_resplot", i, ".png")
  # ggsave(file.path(AIM1_OUTPUT_PATH$figures, filename), res_plot, width = 14, height = 12, dpi = 300)
}
```


# Diagnostics

## Diagnostics plots

```{r diagnostics-plot}
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 14
#| out-width: "100%"

for (i in models$model){
  model_title <- case_when(
    i == "model0" ~ "Model 0: Biomarker ~ Demographics",
    i == "model1" ~ "Model 1: Biomarker ~ Demographics + Genetics",
    i == "model2" ~ "Model 2: Biomarker ~ Demographics + Genetics + Family History",
    i == "model3" ~ "Model 3: Biomarker ~ Demographics + Genetics + Family History + LIBRA2",
    i == "model4" ~ "Model 4: Biomarker ~ Genetics + Family History + CogDrisk",
    TRUE ~ paste("Model:", i)
  )
  
  model_data <- all_preventad %>%
    filter(model == i) %>%
    mutate(
      residuals = map(res, residuals),
      fitted = map(res, fitted)
    ) %>%
    select(outcome, residuals, fitted) %>%
    unnest(c(residuals, fitted))
    
  # Histogram
  p_hist <- ggplot(model_data, aes(x = residuals)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7, color = "white") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    facet_wrap(~outcome, scales = "free_x", ncol = 3) +
    labs(title = "Histogram of Residuals",
         x = "Residuals", y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = NA),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    )
    
  # Q-Q plot
  p_qq <- ggplot(model_data, aes(sample = residuals)) +
    stat_qq(size = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) +
    stat_qq_line(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~outcome, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
    labs(title = "Q-Q Plot",
         x = "Theoretical Quantiles", y = "Sample Quantiles") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = NA),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    )
    
  # Residuals vs Fitted
  p_fitted <- ggplot(model_data, aes(x = fitted, y = residuals)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    geom_smooth(se = FALSE, color = "blue", linewidth = 0.8, method = "loess") +
    facet_wrap(~outcome, scales = "free", ncol = 3) +
    labs(title = "Residuals vs Fitted Values",
         x = "Fitted Values", y = "Residuals") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = NA),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5)
    )
  
  # Stack vertically
  combined <- p_hist / p_qq / p_fitted +
    plot_annotation(
      title = model_title,
      theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
    )
  print(combined)
  
  # Save each plot
  # filename <- paste0("PREVENTAD_diagnostics_", i, ".png")
  # ggsave(file.path(AIM1_OUTPUT_PATH$figures, filename), combined, width = 14, height = 14, dpi = 300)
}
```

- In the CogDrisk model, ptau217, GFAP, and NFL show some slightly curved loess lines which suggest potential non-linear relationships.


## CogDrisk model

```{r}
quadratic_models <- tribble(~model, ~predictors,
                            "model0", "GRS + apoe + family_history + score_cogd",
                            "model1", "age + sex + educ + GRS + apoe",
                            "model2", "age + sex + educ + GRS + apoe + family_history",
                            "model3", "age + sex + educ + apoe + family_history + score_libra2",
                            "model4", "GRS + apoe + family_history + score_cogd") 

cogdrisk_quadratic <- expand_grid(
  outcome = BIOMARKERS_TRANS,
  models
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    eq = glue("{outcome} ~ {predictors}"),
    res = map(eq, ~lm(as.formula(.), data = lastvisit_dat)),
    dataf = map(res, tidy),
    mod = map(res, glance)
  )
# Add quadratic terms for continuous predictors
formula <- biomarker ~ GRS + apoe + family_history + score_cogd + I(score_cogd^2)

# Or use splines for more flexibility
library(splines)
formula <- biomarker ~ GRS + apoe + family_history + ns(score_cogd, df = 3)

```


## Influential points analysis

```{r influential-functions}
# Function to plot Cook's Distance and Leverage
plot_cd <- function(model, outcome_name) {
  cd <- cooks.distance(model)
  lev <- hatvalues(model)
  n <- length(cd)
  p <- length(coef(model))

  # Thresholds
  cd_threshold <- 4 / n  
  lev_threshold <- 2 * p / n  

  # Create data frame for plotting
  diag_df <- tibble(
    obs = 1:n,
    cooks_d = cd,
    leverage = lev,
    influential_cd = cd > cd_threshold,
    influential_lev = lev > lev_threshold
  )

  p1 <- ggplot(diag_df, aes(x = obs, y = cooks_d)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = influential_cd), size = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = cd_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey50", "TRUE" = "red"), guide = "none") +
    labs(title = paste("Cook's Distance:", outcome_name),
         x = "Observation", y = "Cook's Distance") +
    theme_minimal()

  p2 <- ggplot(diag_df, aes(x = obs, y = leverage)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = influential_lev), size = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = lev_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey50", "TRUE" = "red"), guide = "none") +
    labs(title = paste("Leverage:", outcome_name),
         x = "Observation", y = "Leverage") +
    theme_minimal()

  p1 + p2
}
```

### CogDrisk Model

```{r influential-cogd}
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-width: 12
#| results: 'asis'

# Focus on CogDrisk model (model4)
model_cogd <- all_preventad %>% filter(model == "model4")

for (i in 1:nrow(model_cogd)) {
  biomarker <- model_cogd$outcome[i]
  lm_model <- model_cogd$res[[i]]

  cat(paste0("\n\n### ", biomarker, "\n\n"))

  # Get the data used in the model
  dat_model <- model.frame(lm_model)

  n <- nrow(dat_model)
  p <- length(coef(lm_model))

  # Calculate influence measures
  cd <- cooks.distance(lm_model)

  # Thresholds
  cd_threshold <- 4 / n

  # Plot Cook's Distance and Leverage
  print(plot_cd(lm_model, biomarker))

  # Identify influential points using multiple criteria
  influential_cd <- which(cd > cd_threshold)

  cat(paste0("\n**Influential Points Summary:**\n\n"))
  cat(paste0("- Cook's Distance threshold (4/n): ", round(cd_threshold, 4), "\n"))
  cat(paste0("- Points exceeding Cook's D threshold: ", length(influential_cd), "\n"))
  cat(paste0("- Total unique influential points: ", length(influential_cd), " (",
             round(100 * length(influential_cd) / n, 1), "% of sample)\n\n"))
}
```
